ogTitle: Caveton vs Ceduna Waters: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Caveton and Ceduna Waters.
heading: Caveton vs Ceduna Waters — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Caveton, South Australia and Ceduna Waters, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Caveton has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Ceduna Waters (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Caveton or Ceduna Waters safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Caveton has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Ceduna Waters scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Caveton scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Ceduna Waters scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Caveton or Ceduna Waters?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Caveton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
